content.full_text:
COUNTY OF LASSEN                                                     Dawson Arnold
                                                                                County Counsel
County Administrative Center
707 Nevada Street                                          FEB 20 [illegible] '92    William Ballew
Susanville, California 96130                                                       Deputy County Counsel
(916) 237-8311, Ext. 334

TELEFAXED (916) 327-2026


February 18, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 600
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Ms. Blanca Breeze

RE: Request for informal opinion: Lyle Lough

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     In behalf of Mr. Lyle Lough, Chairman of the Board of

Supervisors of Lassen County, an informal opinion is requested

based upon the following facts:

     1.   Mr. Lough owns and operates a water testing

laboratory business within the County of Lassen. Mr. Lough's

laboratory provided water testing services to the California

Department of Corrections for approximately seven years prior to

Mr. Lough's acquisition of it in 1987, and thereafter until June

30, 1991. His contract was on an annual "not to exceed" basis.

The contract is awarded on basis of competitive bidding. He lost

the contract for the 91-92 fiscal year to a lower unitary bidder.

On June 30, 1991, Mr. Lough's business received $5,000.00 (the "not

to exceed" limit) from the California Department of Corrections in

payment of water testing services which the laboratory had

performed during fiscal year 90-91.

     2.   The Department of Corrections presently proposes to

expand the California Correctional Center at Susanville, Lassen


If you could provide a clearer or higher contrast version of this document, I would be able to transcribe it properly following your rules.

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Page 2
February 18, 1992

County, California.  This expansion has become a controversial

issue within the County of Lassen.  The Department of Corrections

has indicated that it will not go forward with the project without

the endorsement of the Lassen County Board of Supervisors.  The

scope and amount of services to be furnished under future water

testing contracts would not be affected by expansion.

     3.  On February 4, 1992, the Board of Supervisors

conducted a public meeting in which Supervisor Lough did not

participate.  A Motion to put future prison expansions on the

ballot for a non-binding advisory vote of the County's electors

failed to receive three affirmative votes.

     4.  A local radio talk show supports the prison

expansion and has broadcast the home phone number of each Lassen

County Supervisor, urging the radio audience to call and voice

their opinions to the several Board members.

     5.  Supervisors Lough has received an unprecedented

number of such calls.  A significant percentage of the callers wish

to have the matter appear on the June 2, 1992 ballot as a county-

wide advisory measure.

     6.  Mr. Lough has received telephonic advise of FPPC

that he is disqualified to vote on direct approval of the proposed

prison expansion until June 30, 1992.

     7.  The last date on which a local measure can be placed

on the ballot to be voted upon at the June 2, 1992 election is

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Page 3
February 18, 1992

March 6, 1992.

     8.  May Mr. Lough participate in a decision to place

the question on the June 2, 1992 ballot to be voted upon as a non-

binding advisory measure by the electors of Lassen County? (Mr.

Lough would not participate in any subsequent decision on the

prison expansion question following said election).

     9.  If the answer is in the negative, may Mr. Lough

participate, prior to June 30, 1992 in a decision to place the

question on the November 1992 ballot as an advisory measure?

     Thank you for your prompt consideration to this matter.

If you have any questions or comments, please do not hesitate to

contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        COUNTY COUNSEL
                                        COUNTY OF LASSEN

                                        [signature]
                                        DAWSON ARNOLD,
                                        County Counsel

DA:lh

sections.question:
on the November 1992 ballot as an advisory measure?
Thank you for your prompt consideration to this matter.
